task: Add input validation to the login() function in src/auth.js so it rejects an empty username with a clear error.

input.query: add input validation to login() to reject empty username

input.title: login() rejects empty username after normalization
input.learning:
login in src/auth.js validates input by normalizing the username first (trim + lowercase via normalizeUsername), then throws Error("username is required") if the resulting key is empty. Checking after normalization (not before) ensures whitespace-only and null/undefined inputs are also rejected, while preserving the existing normalize-first auth convention. The throw happens before db.check, so the DB is never queried for an empty key.</learning>
<parameter name="type">decision
input.evidence: src/auth.js:5 — `if (!key) throw new Error("username is required");` placed between normalizeUsername and db.check.

input.reason: login() now both normalizes the username (original convention, preserved) and rejects empty/whitespace-only usernames after normalization. The replacement packet documents the combined behavior.
